title: The Prince
author: Machiavelli, Niccolò
summary: Niccolò Machiavelli’s The Prince is a concise manual advising rulers how to acquire, govern, and preserve principalities. Drawn from his Florentine service and diplomatic missions, it classifies states, analyses hereditary and new dominions, and recommends military readiness, prudence, and popular support. Machiavelli cites contemporary examples—Cesare Borgia, Lorenzo de’ Medici, Catherina Sforza—and criticizes mercenaries, flatterers, and misplaced faith. He argues that cruelty, clemency, generosity, and the keeping of promises must be judged by political utility. Written after his exile, the work blends historical observation, pragmatic prescriptions, and reflections on fortune and virtue to counsel practical statecraft for ambitious rulers everywhere today.

characters.name: Nicolo Machiavelli
characters.personality: practical, observant, analytical; devoted to historical study and political prescription
characters.lifespan: Florentine official until 1512, imprisoned and exiled, retired to San Casciano and wrote The Prince; died 1527

characters.name: Bernardo di Nicolo Machiavelli
characters.personality: legal professional of some repute; member of old Florentine nobility
characters.lifespan: presented as Machiavelli's father; no further fate described in excerpt

characters.name: Bartolommea di Stefano Nelli
characters.personality: mother from old Florentine nobility
characters.lifespan: mentioned as Machiavelli's mother; no further fate described in excerpt

characters.name: Lorenzo de’ Medici (Il Magnifico)
characters.personality: splendour-loving, influential patronal figure referenced as an exemplar of magnificence
characters.lifespan: his rule is background to Machiavelli's youth; his grandson is patron to whom The Prince was dedicated

characters.name: Savonarola
characters.personality: fervent, austere religious reformer; cited as an unarmed prophet
characters.lifespan: mentioned as having come to a bad end in the text

characters.name: Guido (Machiavelli's son)
characters.age: adult correspondent in letters (exact age not given)
characters.personality: dutiful; advised by his father to study and improve himself
characters.lifespan: mentioned in Machiavelli's advice letter; no further fate described

characters.name: Catherina Sforza (my lady of Forli)
characters.age: contemporary ruler during Machiavelli's service (exact age not given)
characters.personality: vigorous, defended her state; used as an example regarding fortresses versus popular support
characters.lifespan: encountered by Machiavelli on a diplomatic mission; her conduct is used as a political lesson

characters.name: Louis XII
characters.age: ruling king during events described (exact age not given)
characters.personality: monarch whose errors in statecraft are criticized
characters.lifespan: described as having been driven out due to strategic errors

characters.name: Cesare Borgia (Duke Valentino)
characters.age: contemporary military and political actor (exact age not given)
characters.personality: ambitious, ruthless, example of prudence and also of reliance on fortune
characters.lifespan: cited as both exemplary in methods and as a figure who rose and fell with fortune

characters.name: Pius III
characters.age: pontiff who died shortly after election (exact age not given)
characters.personality: briefly mentioned in sequence of papal elections
characters.lifespan: died soon after election; his death precedes election of Julius II

characters.name: Giuliano delle Rovere (Julius II)
characters.age: pontiff during events described (exact age not given)
characters.personality: impetuous, determined, politically active; antagonist to Cesare Borgia
characters.lifespan: elected pope and later ruins Cesare Borgia according to Machiavelli

characters.name: Pope Alexander VI
characters.age: pontiff during events described (exact age not given)
characters.personality: politically opportunistic; father to Cesare Borgia
characters.lifespan: central to political maneuvers described in Machiavelli's career

characters.name: Filippo Casavecchio
characters.personality: friend and reader who discussed Machiavelli's draft
characters.lifespan: consulted by Machiavelli about The Prince; mentioned as having seen it

characters.name: Francesco Vettori
characters.personality: friend and correspondent who received letters describing Machiavelli's life
characters.lifespan: recipient of Machiavelli's famous letter describing his study routine; remains a correspondent

characters.name: Dante
characters.personality: author cited by Machiavelli for the value of retained learning
characters.lifespan: quoted in Machiavelli's letter; historical authority

characters.name: Ferdinand of Aragon
characters.age: ruling monarch during described events (exact age not given)
characters.personality: portrayed as religious cloak for ambition; pragmatic and unmerciful
characters.lifespan: character sketched as successful through calculated ruthlessness

characters.name: Emperor Maximilian
characters.age: ruling sovereign during period (exact age not given)
characters.personality: secretive, lacking force of character according to Machiavelli
characters.lifespan: described as failing often because of secretiveness and lack of insistence
